name: Mirador do Coto da Pena – circular from Cortegada
name_original: Mirador do Coto da Pena – circular desde Cortegada
region_intro: Jogging routes around Crecente Galicia offer diverse terrain through verdant landscapes, ancient forests, and along the majestic River Miño. The municipality is characterized by beautiful valleys and mountainous zones, providing varied gradients for running. These natural features create a picturesque setting for outdoor activities, with paths suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Crecente The most popular jogging route is Mirador do Coto da Pena – circular from Cortegada, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the Coto da Pena. Another top favourite among local runners is Laboreiro River Waterfalls loop from Arbo, a difficult 44.5 miles (71.6 km) path. This extensive route follows the Laboreiro River, featuring scenic waterfalls and varied riverside terrain. Local runners also love the Running loop from Melgaço, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Crecente is defined by the River Miño, lush valleys, and ancient forests, with options ranging from easy paths to challenging routes through mountainous zones. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Crecente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Crecente?
A: Crecente offers a selection of &lt;em&gt;over 10 running routes&lt;/em&gt;, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes traverse the region's beautiful valleys, ancient forests, and areas near the River Miño.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Crecente?
A: Running trails in Crecente feature diverse terrain. You'll find paths through lush valleys and ancient forests, as well as mountainous zones offering varied gradients. Some sections along the River Miño are known to be paved and well-maintained, while other trails, like those leading to pools, might be stone paths.

Q: Are there any running routes in Crecente that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, Crecente and its surroundings boast several beautiful waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31543181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laboreiro River Waterfalls loop from Arbo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that follows the Laboreiro River, featuring scenic waterfalls. You can also find natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6067935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.www.komoot.com/highlight/5268566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourón Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging routes in Crecente?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, Crecente's network includes easy and moderate paths through valleys and forests. The municipality highlights local walking routes in Filgueira and Vilar, which are generally suitable for nature lovers and may offer gentler options for families.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Crecente?
A: Many of Crecente's natural trails through forests and along riverbanks are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. Always check local regulations and ensure your dog is comfortable with the terrain and distance.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might see while jogging in Crecente?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you might encounter historical sites and other points of interest. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1733080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grande Hotel do Pezo Ruins&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1267491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Melgaço&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrelo de Miño Nautical Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Termas de Prexigueiro&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Crecente?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990558063/mirador-do-coto-da-pena-circular-from-cortegada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador do Coto da Pena – circular from Cortegada&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31539897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourón Waterfalls loop from Melón&lt;/a&gt;, providing a scenic experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Crecente?
A: Crecente's verdant landscape makes it appealing for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the ancient forests provide shaded routes. Winter runs are possible, though some trails might be wetter.

Q: Are there options for challenging runs with significant elevation gain in Crecente?
A: Absolutely. The northern part of Crecente features mountainous zones, providing varied gradients for more challenging runs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990558063/mirador-do-coto-da-pena-circular-from-cortegada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador do Coto da Pena – circular from Cortegada&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and panoramic views, ideal for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout.

Q: How accessible are the running routes in Crecente by public transport or car?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, Crecente is a municipality in Pontevedra, Galicia, generally accessible by car. Many routes will have parking options nearby. For detailed public transport information, it's advisable to check local bus schedules for the specific starting points of your chosen route.

Q: Can I find running paths along the River Miño in Crecente?
A: The majestic River Miño is a defining feature of Crecente, and its river basin offers excellent opportunities for jogging. River paths and trails along the Miño are available, with some sections known to be paved and well-maintained, providing a picturesque setting for a run.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Crecente?
A: Yes, Crecente offers routes suitable for different ability levels, including easy options. The municipality's lush valleys and ancient forests provide gentler paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Prado e Remoães&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that could be a good starting point for those looking for less challenging terrain.

Q: Where can I find refreshments or places to relax after a run in Crecente?
A: Crecente, being a rural Galician municipality, has local establishments where you can find refreshments. While specific cafes or pubs directly on trails are not listed, the villages and towns within the municipality, such as Filgueira and Vilar, will offer options for a post-run drink or meal.
tips: Located in the thermal town of Cortegada, on the banks of the Miño River, is the church of Santa María. It is a 19th century neo-Gothic temple built by the architect Daniel Vázquez Gulín.
Near the old Cortegada Spa we can enjoy the magnificent views of the Miño river valley. From the viewpoint you can see the calm waters, a few kilometers before the Frieira reservoir.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Cortegada
